title: US Beat Senegal 3-2 in Final Friendly — Pochettino Optimistic
excerpt: The United States Men's National Team defeated Senegal 3-2 in their final pre-World Cup friendly at Arrowhead Stadium, with head coach Mauricio Pochettino expressing optimism ahead of Group D play.
content:
## A Striking Performance

The USMNT closed out their World Cup preparation with a hard-fought 3-2 victory over Senegal at Arrowhead Stadium in Kansas City. Goals from Christian Pulisic (12'), Folarin Balogun (44'), and Weston McKennie (67') secured the win in front of a sold-out crowd of 76,000 fans.

Senegal fought back with goals from Sadio Mané (34') and Ismaila Sarr (81'), but the American defense held firm in the closing minutes.

## Pochettino's Assessment

"I am very proud of the team's performance," said head coach Mauricio Pochettino in the post-match press conference. "Senegal is a strong opponent — they reached the Round of 16 in 2022. To beat them with this intensity and tactical discipline is very encouraging."

When asked about Group D — where the US faces Australia, Paraguay, and Turkey — Pochettino was direct: "We respect every opponent, but we believe we can win the group. The objective is clear: advance and go deep."

## Group D Outlook

The United States opens their World Cup campaign on June 12 against Australia at AT&T Stadium in Dallas. Key matches follow:
- **June 14:** United States vs Paraguay at Arrowhead Stadium (Kansas City)
- **June 21:** United States vs Turkey at AT&T Stadium (Dallas)

## VIP Perspective

For fans attending US matches, Vantage 26 offers premium hospitality packages at all Group D venues. At AT&T Stadium (94,000 capacity), our Presidential Suites provide unparalleled comfort with private catering, personal concierges, and direct access from Rolls Royce drop-off zones.

## What's Next

The USMNT will travel directly to Dallas following the friendly, establishing their training base at the Dallas Cowboys' training facility in Frisco, Texas. The adjusted schedule gives the squad six full days of preparation before their opening match against Australia.
